title: AuditResult
description: Final classification of a completed insert_me run. Captures whether the run produced a valid, meaningful vulnerability case (VALID), a no-op that inserted nothing meaningful (NOOP), a case with unclear or conflicting signals (AMBIGUOUS), or an explicitly rejected case (INVALID). The classification is primarily deterministic (rule-based) and may optionally include LLM-assisted evidence.

audit_id.description: Identifier for this audit result, derived deterministically from run_id.

classification.description:
The primary classification of this run's output.

- VALID: The run produced a structurally sound, plausible, non-trivial vulnerability instance. Safe to include in a labelled corpus.
- NOOP: The run completed but the applied mutation produced no meaningful semantic change (e.g. dead code, guarded by an always-false condition).
- AMBIGUOUS: Conflicting signals — e.g. the mutation is structurally sound but semantic plausibility is uncertain. Requires human review before corpus inclusion.
- INVALID: The run produced output that fails structural checks or is clearly not a real vulnerability instance. Should not be included in a corpus.

confidence.description:
Confidence level in the classification.

- high: Multiple strong signals agree.
- medium: Signals agree but some checks were skipped or had low coverage.
- low: Few signals available, classification may be incorrect.

evidence.description: Ordered list of evidence items supporting the classification. Each item represents one signal or observation.

observation.description: Plain-text description of what was observed.

weight.description: How strongly this evidence supports or undermines the classification.

detail.description: Optional structured detail for machine consumption.

reviewer.description: Who or what produced this audit result.

name.description: Name or identifier of the reviewer (e.g. 'rule_engine_v1', 'claude-3-7', 'alice@example.com').

model_version.description: Model version string if reviewer type is 'llm'.

audited_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the audit was produced.

notes.description: Free-text notes, especially for AMBIGUOUS cases requiring human follow-up.
